title: osn-interface
text: R package querying OpenSky Network (OSN) state vector data via DuckDB, reading parquet files directly OSN S3-compatible bucket.

title: osn-interface
text: Provide lightweight R functions lazily query OpenSky Network historical state vector data without downloading bulk files. package connects DuckDB directly OSN S3 endpoint (s3.opensky-network.org) using httpfs extension, enabling predicate pushdown lazy evaluation remote parquet files.

title: osn-interface
text: OpenSky also exposes Trino endpoint (trino.opensky-network.org), direct S3 access via DuckDB chosen : - Java dependency (Trino requires RJDBC + JVM) - query timeouts concurrency limits (Trino: 30min timeout, 2 concurrent queries) - Trino uses browser-based external authentication (hard automate); S3 uses simple key/secret - DuckDB provides lazy evaluation filter projection pushdown remote parquet - Simpler dependency chain: just duckdb R package

title: osn-interface
text: S3 Endpoint: s3.opensky-network.org Bucket: ec-datadump File pattern: s3://ec-datadump/YYYY-MM-DD/HH/states_YYYY-MM-DD-HH.parquet (24 hourly files per day, hour subdirectory) Credentials: Environment variables OSN_USERNAME (access key ID) OSN_KEY (secret access key)

title: osn-interface
text: File: reference/airports.csv Lookup key: ident column (ICAO code, e.g., "EHAM", "EGLL") Coordinates: latitude_deg, longitude_deg

title: osn-interface
text: osn_connect() — Create configure DuckDB connection S3/httpfs pointed OSN bucket. Reads credentials env vars. osn_fetch_day(date = Sys.Date() - 1, con = NULL) — Lazily fetch state vectors specific day (default: yesterday, D-1). Returns lazy tbl (dbplyr) can filtered collection. osn_filter_radius(lazy_tbl, lat, lon, radius_nm) — Given lazy table, filter state vectors within radius (Nautical Miles) given coordinates. Uses bounding-box pre-filter + Haversine refinement. osn_airport_coords(ident) — Look airport coordinates reference/airports.csv ICAO identifier. Returns list lat lon. osn_fetch_around_airport(ident, radius_nm, date = Sys.Date() - 1, con = NULL) — Convenience wrapper: fetches day’s data filtered radius around named airport.

title: osn-interface
code: dbExecute(con, "INSTALL httpfs; LOAD httpfs;")   # S3 remote parquet access dbExecute(con, "INSTALL spatial; LOAD spatial;")  # ST_Distance_Sphere (Haversine) dbExecute(con, "SET s3_endpoint='s3.opensky-network.org';") dbExecute(con, "SET s3_url_style='path';") dbExecute(con, "SET s3_access_key_id=<from OSN_USERNAME>;") dbExecute(con, "SET s3_secret_access_key=<from OSN_KEY>;")

title: osn-interface
text: Radius specified Nautical Miles (NM) 1 NM = 1852 m = 1/60 degree latitude Uses bounding-box pre-filter (fast, pushed parquet row-group pruning) ST_Distance_Sphere(ST_Point(lon, lat), ST_Point(ref_lon, ref_lat)) DuckDB’s spatial extension precise Haversine great-circle distance (returns meters) steps run inside DuckDB’s engine via dbplyr lazy evaluation

title: osn-interface
text: osn_fetch_day() returns dbplyr lazy table backed read_parquet() S3 glob data fetched user calls collect(), head(), similar Predicate pushdown: clauses parquet columns skip irrelevant row groups Projection pushdown: selected columns transferred

title: osn-interface
text: duckdb (>= 1.0.0) — database engine + httpfs extension DBI — database interface dplyr — data manipulation dbplyr — lazy table translation SQL

title: osn-interface
text: OSN_USERNAME — OpenSky Network S3 access key ID OSN_KEY — OpenSky Network S3 secret access key

title: osn-interface
text: Standard R package structure (DESCRIPTION, NAMESPACE, roxygen2 docs) Keep functions minimal — unnecessary abstractions SQL generation goes dbplyr possible; raw SQL necessary Include installation instructions README.md

what: Query OpenSky Network State Vectors via DuckDB
title: Query OpenSky Network State Vectors via DuckDB
text: EUROCONTROL R package (internal use) lazily querying OpenSky Network historical state vector data via DuckDB. Reads parquet files directly OSN S3 bucket — bulk downloads needed.

title: Query OpenSky Network State Vectors via DuckDB
text: DuckDB’s httpfs extension connects directly OpenSky S3-compatible storage (s3.opensky-network.org) reads parquet files demand. Queries lazy: data transferred call collect(). spatial extension provides native Haversine distance (ST_Distance_Sphere) geographic filtering.

title: Query OpenSky Network State Vectors via DuckDB
text: package can read OpenSky state-vector data two interchangeable sources, selected connection osn_connect(source = ...): sources return lazy dbplyr tables identical schema API — fetch functions read chosen source connection, downstream code (osn_filter_radius(), collect(), …) either way. default remains "osn-ec-datadump", existing code unaffected. "osn-historical-trino" source queries Trino via native R Trino client RPresto, pulls result R, registers DuckDB lazy table. Install dependencies install.packages(c("RPresto", "httr", "jsonlite")). Authentication: OpenSky’s Trino endpoint uses OAuth2 external (browser) authentication — flow trino --external-authentication. first time connect session, browser window opens log OpenSky account; resulting bearer token cached disk (tools::R_user_dir("osninterface", "cache")) reused expires, subsequent connections non-interactive. Basic username/password auth accepted OSN Trino server. Performance: unfiltered day state vectors large, always constrain query. osn_fetch_around_airport() pushes airport bounding box Trino clause automatically (partition column hour plus lat/lon), keeping fetches fast (e.g. EHAM 10 NM one day ≈ 30 s). time %% 5 downsample likewise applied server-side. default fetch functions downsample one state vector every 5 seconds (time %% 5 == 0), matching pyopensky. Override downsample_s argument (e.g. downsample_s = 1 NULL keep every row).

title: Query OpenSky Network State Vectors via DuckDB
text: R >= 4.1.0 OpenSky Network account S3 data access (typically university-affiliated researchers, governmental organisations, aviation authorities)

title: Query OpenSky Network State Vectors via DuckDB
text: need OpenSky Network account historical-data access (request https://opensky-network.org/-opensky/request-data). Depending source use: osn-ec-datadump (default, S3): S3 access key ID + secret key pair. look like k4d8f2... (≈16 chars) longer secret (≈32 chars). go OSN_USERNAME OSN_KEY. osn-historical-trino (Trino): OpenSky account username, used tag OPENSKY_USERNAME. put password — Trino source logs browser (OAuth2). See Data sources.

what: 2. Install the package and (optionally) the Trino dependencies

what: 3. Create a .env file with your credentials
title: Query OpenSky Network State Vectors via DuckDB
text: project root (working directory), copy template fill : edit .env looks like (fill values): .env git-ignored must never committed. need fill credentials source(s) intend use.
code: cp .env.template .env # --- osn-ec-datadump source (default) --- OSN_USERNAME=your_s3_access_key_id OSN_KEY=your_s3_secret_access_key  # --- osn-historical-trino source --- OPENSKY_USERNAME=your_opensky_login   # tag only; auth is via browser OPENSKY_PASSWORD=                      # leave blank — not used by Trino OAuth2

title: Query OpenSky Network State Vectors via DuckDB
text: use Trino source instead, connect osn_connect(source = "osn-historical-trino"); browser window opens first use log (token cached — see Authentication). Tip: prefer use .env file, set variables via ~/.Renviron shell — see Configuration .
code: library(osninterface) library(dplyr)  osn_load_env()            # load credentials from ./.env  con <- osn_connect()      # default source = "osn-ec-datadump"  # One day of traffic within 40 NM of Amsterdam Schiphol (D-1 by default) sv  <- osn_fetch_around_airport("EHAM", radius_nm = 40, con = con) df  <- sv |> collect()    # data transfers only now (lazy until collect)  head(df) osn_disconnect(con)

title: Query OpenSky Network State Vectors via DuckDB
code: library(osninterface) library(dplyr)  # (optional) load credentials from a project .env file osn_load_env()  # Connect to the default S3 (ec-datadump) source con <- osn_connect(proxy = TRUE) # proxy = TRUE on ECTL HP laptop / proxy = FALSE on ECTL Macbook  # ...or use the historical Trino source instead # con <- osn_connect(source = "osn-historical-trino")  # Airport lookup shorthand (defaults to 5-second updates) sv_eham <- osn_fetch_around_airport("EHAM", radius_nm = 40, con = con)  # Keep every state vector instead of downsampling sv_full <- osn_fetch_around_airport("EHAM", radius_nm = 40, con = con, downsample_s = NULL)  # Only now does data actually transfer result <- sv_eham |> collect()  # Always disconnect when done osn_disconnect(con)

title: Query OpenSky Network State Vectors via DuckDB
text: Lazy table single day (default: yesterday, D-1).
code: con <- osn_connect() sv  <- osn_fetch_day(date = "2024-06-01", con = con) sv |> filter(!onGround) |> head(100) |> collect()  # keep every state vector (no 5-second downsample) sv_full <- osn_fetch_day("2024-06-01", con = con, downsample_s = NULL)

title: Query OpenSky Network State Vectors via DuckDB
text: Lazy table spanning inclusive date range.
code: con <- osn_connect() sv  <- osn_fetch_days(from = "2024-06-01", to = "2024-06-03", con = con) sv |> summarise(n = n()) |> collect()

title: Query OpenSky Network State Vectors via DuckDB
text: Filter lazy table radius (Nautical Miles) around point. Combines fast bounding-box pre-filter precise Haversine distance filter.
code: con  <- osn_connect() lhr  <- osn_airport_coords("EGLL") near <- osn_fetch_day("2024-06-01", con = con) |>   osn_filter_radius(lat = lhr$lat, lon = lhr$lon, radius_nm = 30) near |> collect()

title: Query OpenSky Network State Vectors via DuckDB
text: Convenience wrapper: fetch day filter radius around named airport one call. Pushes bounding box query speed.
code: con <- osn_connect() sv  <- osn_fetch_around_airport("LFPG", radius_nm = 25,                                 date = "2024-06-01", con = con) sv |> collect()

title: Query OpenSky Network State Vectors via DuckDB

code: tcon <- osn_trino_connect() DBI::dbGetQuery(tcon, "SELECT count(*) FROM minio.osky.state_vectors_data4                        WHERE hour = 1717200000") DBI::dbDisconnect(tcon)

what: Create a DuckDB connection configured for OpenSky Network S3 — osn_connect
title: Create a DuckDB connection configured for OpenSky Network S3 — osn_connect
text: Initialises DuckDB connection, installs/loads httpfs spatial extensions, configures S3 access OSN bucket using OSN_USERNAME OSN_KEY environment variables.

title: Create a DuckDB connection configured for OpenSky Network S3 — osn_connect
code: osn_connect(   proxy = FALSE,   extension_directory = NULL,   source = c("osn-ec-datadump", "osn-historical-trino") )

title: Create a DuckDB connection configured for OpenSky Network S3 — osn_connect
text: proxy Logical. TRUE, configures DuckDB route corporate HTTP proxy. Proxy URL password parsed HTTPS_PROXY environment variable (expected format: http://user:password@host:port). proxy username taken Windows session username. Extensions force-installed local directory additional extensions (ui, h3) installed. extension_directory Optional. Path directory containing DuckDB extensions. specified, DuckDB use local extensions instead downloading . Can also set via DUCKDB_EXTENSION_DIRECTORY environment variable. Useful corporate environments restricted internet access. specified environment variable set, DuckDB download extensions needed (current default behavior). source Data source use subsequent fetches. One : "osn-ec-datadump" (default) — read parquet directly OSN S3 bucket (s3.opensky-network.org) using OSN_USERNAME / OSN_KEY credentials. original behaviour. "osn-historical-trino" — read underlying OSN data OSN historical Trino endpoint (trino.opensky-network.org), backend used pyopensky / traffic Python libraries, authenticated OPENSKY_USERNAME / OPENSKY_PASSWORD credentials. Requires RPresto httr packages. sources return lazy dbplyr tables; fetch functions read choice connection. chosen source stored connection (see osn_source()) osn_fetch_*() functions know backend use without extra argument.

title: Create a DuckDB connection configured for OpenSky Network S3 — osn_connect
text: DBI connection object selected data source recorded attribute.

title: Create a DuckDB connection configured for OpenSky Network S3 — osn_connect
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Use local extensions (method 1: parameter) con <- osn_connect(extension_directory = "~/dev/duckdb_ext")  # Use local extensions (method 2: environment variable) Sys.setenv(DUCKDB_EXTENSION_DIRECTORY = "~/dev/duckdb_ext") con <- osn_connect()  # Default S3 (ec-datadump) source con <- osn_connect()  # Secondary historical Trino source con <- osn_connect(source = "osn-historical-trino") } # }

what: Fetch state vectors around an airport — osn_fetch_around_airport
title: Fetch state vectors around an airport — osn_fetch_around_airport
text: Convenience wrapper combines osn_fetch_day(), osn_airport_coords(), osn_filter_radius(). airport bounding box pushed fetch (server-side "osn-historical-trino" source, nearby rows transferred), refined precise Haversine radius filter.

title: Fetch state vectors around an airport — osn_fetch_around_airport
code: osn_fetch_around_airport(   ident,   radius_nm,   date = Sys.Date() - 1,   con = NULL,   downsample_s = 5 )

title: Fetch state vectors around an airport — osn_fetch_around_airport
text: ident ICAO airport code (e.g. "EHAM"). radius_nm Radius Nautical Miles. date Date fetch. Default: yesterday. con DBI connection osn_connect(). NULL, new connection created automatically. downsample_s Optional integer. Keep state vectors whose Unix time multiple many seconds (time %% downsample_s == 0). Default 5. Set NULL, 0, 1 keep every row.

title: Fetch state vectors around an airport — osn_fetch_around_airport
text: Filtered lazy tbl_dbi.

what: Lazily fetch state vectors for a single day — osn_fetch_day
title: Lazily fetch state vectors for a single day — osn_fetch_day
text: Returns lazy dbplyr table one day's state vectors. data transferred call dplyr::collect(), head(), similar. source recorded con (see osn_connect()) selects backend:

title: Lazily fetch state vectors for a single day — osn_fetch_day
code: osn_fetch_day(date = Sys.Date() - 1, con = NULL, downsample_s = 5, bbox = NULL)

title: Lazily fetch state vectors for a single day — osn_fetch_day
text: date Date fetch. Default: yesterday (Sys.Date() - 1). con DBI connection osn_connect(). NULL, new connection created automatically. downsample_s Optional integer. Keep state vectors whose Unix time multiple many seconds (time %% downsample_s == 0), reducing update rate. Default 5 (5-second updates), matching pyopensky's behaviour. Set NULL, 0, 1 keep every row. bbox Optional bounding box (list lat_min, lat_max, lon_min, lon_max) pre-filter server-side. "osn-historical-trino" source pushed Trino query rows inside box transferred — important performance, unfiltered day large. Ignored "osn-ec-datadump" source (prunes via later osn_filter_radius() parquet pushdown). Usually set indirectly via osn_fetch_around_airport().

title: Lazily fetch state vectors for a single day — osn_fetch_day
text: lazy tbl_dbi (dbplyr table).

title: Lazily fetch state vectors for a single day — osn_fetch_day
text: "osn-ec-datadump" — DuckDB view remote parquet files OSN S3 bucket. "osn-historical-trino" — day queried OSN Trino endpoint registered DuckDB lazy table. Either way returned object lazy tbl_dbi identical schema.

Set NULL, 0, 1 keep every row. bbox Optional bounding box (see osn_fetch_day()) pushed Trino query "osn-historical-trino" source.

title: Filter state vectors to a radius around a point — osn_filter_radius

what: Open a connection to the OpenSky Network Trino endpoint — osn_trino_connect
title: Open a connection to the OpenSky Network Trino endpoint — osn_trino_connect
text: Connects trino.opensky-network.org (catalog minio, schema osky) using native R Trino client RPresto. OpenSky's Trino endpoint uses OAuth2 external authentication (browser-based flow trino --external-authentication): first use browser window opens log OpenSky account; resulting bearer token cached disk reused expires. backend used "osn-historical-trino" source (see osn_connect()).

title: Open a connection to the OpenSky Network Trino endpoint — osn_trino_connect
code: osn_trino_connect(force_login = FALSE)

title: Open a connection to the OpenSky Network Trino endpoint — osn_trino_connect
text: force_login Logical. TRUE, ignore cached token force fresh browser login.

title: Open a connection to the OpenSky Network Trino endpoint — osn_trino_connect
text: PrestoConnection (DBI connection) OSN Trino server.

title: Open a connection to the OpenSky Network Trino endpoint — osn_trino_connect
text: OPENSKY_USERNAME environment variable used Trino user tag (X-Trino-User); actual authentication happens via browser login, OPENSKY_PASSWORD. Requires RPresto, httr jsonlite packages (Suggests); install install.packages(c("RPresto", "httr", "jsonlite")) missing.

title: osninterface 0.2.0
text: Second data source. osn_connect() gains source argument selecting backend, stored connection read osn_fetch_*() functions: "osn-ec-datadump" (default) — original path: DuckDB reads parquet directly OSN S3 bucket (OSN_USERNAME / OSN_KEY). "osn-historical-trino" — OSN historical Trino endpoint (trino.opensky-network.org, catalog minio, schema osky), backend used pyopensky / traffic Python libraries. Results pulled R registered DuckDB, sources return identical lazy dbplyr tables downstream code unchanged. Trino OAuth2 authentication. Trino source authenticates via OpenSky’s OAuth2 external (browser) flow — trino --external-authentication. browser login required first use per token; bearer token cached disk reused expires. New exported helper osn_trino_connect() opens raw Trino connection. Temporal downsampling. osn_fetch_day(), osn_fetch_days() osn_fetch_around_airport() gain downsample_s argument (default 5) keeping state vectors time %% downsample_s == 0 (e.g. one update every 5 seconds), matching pyopensky. Set NULL, 0 1 keep every row. Applied server-side sources. Bounding-box pushdown. osn_fetch_around_airport() pushes airport bounding box query (server-side Trino source, alongside required hour partition filter), nearby rows transferred precise Haversine radius refinement. New helpers: osn_source() (report connection’s source) osn_load_env() (load credentials local .env file). .env.template documents credentials sources.

title: osninterface 0.1.1
text: osn_connect() now supports specifying local extension directory via extension_directory parameter DUCKDB_EXTENSION_DIRECTORY environment variable. allows using local DuckDB extensions instead downloading , particularly useful corporate environments restricted internet access (#XX). Extension loading now tries LOAD INSTALL non-proxy mode, automatically using local extensions available falling back download needed.

title: osninterface 0.1.1
text: Fixed issue osn_connect() fail corporate environments due blocked extension downloads, even extensions existed locally.

title: osninterface 0.1.0
text: Initial release
